mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and rising aggregate income, which feeds into consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS rises from one month to the next and the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 45% of such instances over any rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8%, it indicates significant labor market slack and rising economic distress, which depresses consumer confidence and reduces expected corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% in May 2013 is below the 8% threshold
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE exceeds 8%, if the average 20-day SPX return is non-negative (>=0) over the following month, across at least 10 distinct episodes, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS near zero) and rising housing starts (HOUST) reflects accommodative monetary policy transmitting into housing activity, a powerful stimulus that boosts construction, employment, and consumer wealth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods with FEDFUNDS below 1% and HOUST above 800, the 20-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time over any two-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In the early-cycle recovery regime following a deep recession, rising industrial production (INDPRO) confirms that supply chains are healing and capacity is being rebuilt, which is unambiguously positive for forward equity returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
evidence_refs: fact:decision date 2013-06-15 is in early-cycle recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery (first 3 years after NBER trough)
failure_condition: If, during periods classified as early-cycle recovery, a positive monthly INDPRO change is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden acceleration in consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and short-term earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS monthly increase >0.5% is followed by a negative 5-day SPX return in more than 40% of such occurrences over any 1-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) are rising month-over-month simultaneously, the economy is experiencing a synchronized expansion in income and spending, which is a more powerful positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive monthly change
failure_condition: If in months where both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase MoM, the 20-day SPX return is negative in at least 40% of such months over any 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth on a year-over-year basis proxies the fundamental pace of economic output; a rising trend indicates that demand is strengthening, which supports corporate earnings growth and justifies higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between trailing 4-quarter GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime where the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year change exceeds 3%, further rises in CPI signal overheating that forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during high inflation regime (CPI YoY > 3%) a positive monthly CPI print is followed by a positive 20-day SPX return in more than 30% of instances over any 2-year period, reject the hypothesis.
